description:
You are a platform-engineer who has just joined a team that runs several
    GitLab-CI jobs in a containerised build farm.  
    Your first assignment is to prove that you can capture and normalise the
    Python package requirements that will later be baked into the production
    image.

    IMPORTANT – the automated grader will only look at the **two** files
    listed below and nothing else, so make sure the exact names, paths, line
    order and byte-for-byte contents are correct.  All paths are assumed to be
    under the normal unprivileged account (`/home/user`).

    1. Create the working directory tree  
       /home/user/ci_cd/  
       └── logs/                 (a sub-directory for log artefacts)

    2. Inside /home/user/ci_cd/ create a text file called
       requirements.input with **exactly** the following three lines
       (both package names and versions are mandatory; they must be lower-case
       and use the double-equals version pin):

            requests==2.31.0
            pyyaml==6.0.1
            pytest==8.1.1

       Do **not** add extra blank lines or comments.

    3. Pretend that these three lines came from an external feed that may be
       unordered.  Produce a *sorted* version called  
       /home/user/ci_cd/pinned_requirements_sorted.txt  
       that contains the same three lines but strictly in **alphabetical order
       by the package name** (A→Z) and nothing else.  
       The required final byte-for-byte content is therefore:

            pyyaml==6.0.1
            pytest==8.1.1
            requests==2.31.0

    4. For traceability in the pipeline, write an audit log file called  
       /home/user/ci_cd/logs/pip_audit.log  
       with the following fixed format – every line is required and must
       appear exactly once, in this order, with a single LF line ending:

            PKG_AUDIT_START
            pyyaml:6.0.1:OK
            pytest:8.1.1:OK
            requests:2.31.0:OK
            PKG_AUDIT_END

       • Do not include surrounding spaces.  
       • The package lines must be sorted **alphabetically by package name**
         (same order as in the sorted requirements file).  
       • The literal word “OK” must be present after each version, separated by
         a single colon.

    5. You are encouraged (but not forced) to create and activate a
       Python virtual-environment somewhere under /home/user/tmp and to run
       `pip install -r requirements.input` to verify things locally, but the
       grader will not check the virtual-environment itself – only the two
       files mentioned above will be validated.

    When you finish, both files must exist and have the exact contents
    specified so the automated test can perform a byte-for-byte comparison.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth used by the autograder.  These values must remain hidden
        from the agent.
    -->

    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/ci_cd/pinned_requirements_sorted.txt"><![CDATA[
pyyaml==6.0.1
pytest==8.1.1
requests==2.31.0
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/ci_cd/logs/pip_audit.log"><![CDATA[
PKG_AUDIT_START
pyyaml:6.0.1:OK
pytest:8.1.1:OK
requests:2.31.0:OK
PKG_AUDIT_END
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
